assertions.description: SpeechRecognition or webkitSpeechRecognition exists in window.

assertions.test: (() => { const SR = window.SpeechRecognition || window.webkitSpeechRecognition; if (!SR) return false; return 'unspokenPunctuation' in SR.prototype; })()

assertions.description: The unspokenPunctuation property defaults to false.
assertions.test: (() => { const SR = window.SpeechRecognition || window.webkitSpeechRecognition; if (!SR) return false; const r = new SR(); return r.unspokenPunctuation === false; })()

assertions.description: The unspokenPunctuation property can be set to true and it sticks.
assertions.test: (() => { const SR = window.SpeechRecognition || window.webkitSpeechRecognition; if (!SR) return false; const r = new SR(); r.unspokenPunctuation = true; return r.unspokenPunctuation === true; })()
